title: At least 8 people injured in Level-1 fire in Mumbai’s Andheri
content: According to the Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation, the fire at Manisha Building near Arogya Nidhi Hospital in Juhu, Andheri West, was confined to the AC unit on the 6th floor of the 11-storey building

title: Somewhere India lost the plot with the African Union: Ambassador Gurjit Singh
content: The former Indian Envoy to the African Union came days after the fourth India-Africa Forum Summit, scheduled for May 31, was cancelled due to Ebola outbreaks across Africa

title: Blueprint for first phase of PM MITRA Textile Park in Kalaburagi is ready
content: The park will be equipped with underground high-tension and low-tension power distribution lines, and energy-efficient solar street lighting

title: Nipah virus: Containment steps on in Kerala’s Kozhikode after man tests positive for disease
content: Seventy-seven persons on patient’s contact list, 15 people quarantined. Health department working in coordination with departments of Forest and Wildlife and Animal Husbandry

title: When monsoon clogs Hyderabad’s tech heart
content: A billion-year-old terrain, a 25-year-old master plan and decades of urbanisation later, the city’s IT corridor still dreads the rain. On June 9, a brief but intense downpour exposed the fault lines beneath Cyberabad’s gleaming skyline, trapping more than three lakh vehicles in a massive gridlock and raising fresh questions about drainage, planning and infrastructure. Serish Nanisetti, Siddharth Kumar Singh and Lavpreet Kaur decode why Cyberabad remains so vulnerable to flooding

title: Ernakulam on alert following rise in shigellosis cases in State
content: While no cases have been reported recently in the district, the threat remains, says DMO; public urged to follow food safety precautions and maintain personal hygiene

title: Syringe recall at AIIMS was a precautionary, patient-safety measure: Health Ministry
content: In a letter written to Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda, Rajya Sabha MP Haris Beeran had said that in the past month two separate recall notices were issued for disposable 10 ml syringes with needles supplied by different manufacturers, citing quality-related complaints

title: Haryana accepts key Anganwadi workers’ demands after protests: CITU
content: Following the protests, a union delegation held a nearly three-hour meeting with the government chaired by Additional Chief Secretary Shekhar Vidyarthi at the Irrigation Department Headquarters in Panchkula

title: New Collectorate building will be inaugurated by CM in Karimnagar by month-end: Ponnam Prabhakar
content: The helipad on the premises of the new Collectorate complex will be operationalised soon, Mr. Prabhakar said

title: Surface coal gasification makes a strong case for business: Kishan Reddy to investors
content: Coal gasification is closely linked to the future of India’s manufacturing sector, especially energy, fertilizer and steel, the Union Minister said, asserting India is ready to enter coal gasification in a big way in a decade

title: Renewable energy producers look for policy changes from T.N. government
content: A solar energy project goes through seven stages, from land acquisition to commissioning, and takes about three months; the process should be made seamless, say investors

title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Messi, Neymar and Ronaldo are now on Kerala’s mundus
content: As World Cup fever sweeps Kerala, designers and stores are creating customised mundus, lungis, and wraps featuring star football players and popular team colours for fans. Here is how you can get your wardrobe ready, to cheer on your favourite players

title: U.P. Police Constable Exam: When hope and desperation collide
content: With over 28 lakh youths applying for a little over 32,000 constable-rank positions in Uttar Pradesh, the focus is back on employment opportunities and the increasing number of young degree holders in the State, reports Mayank Kumar

title: No guarantee of right to work?
content: Hailed as one of the most important social welfare laws for making the State accountable for providing dignified livelihoods, MGNREGA 2005, is set to be replaced by VB-G RAM G, 2025. Sections of workers argue that the new Act strips the scheme of its core promise, the Right to Work backed by a guarantee, writes Rishita Khanna

title: India expected to be among the countries invited to join initiative to secure Strait of Hormuz: French sources
content: All issues including West Asia will be discussed when PM Modi meets President Macron, says MEA's Secretary (West) Sibi George

title: U.S. Navy attacked 3 merchant vessels with Indian seafarers; these strikes must stop: India's firm message to Washington
content: We attach high importance to the welfare and well-being of our seafaring community. When this particular attack on the ship Settebello occurred, we lodged a strong protest with the American side, MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said

title: Himachal wants high level committee to assess financial impact; Punjab wants Special Category Status
content: They were speaking at the 11th Governing Council meeting of NITI Aayog held under the theme ‘Inclusive human development for Viksit Bharat’, chaired by the Prime Minister in New Delhi.

title: No undue interference, no support to divisive politics, says DCM Sunetra Pawar in her first foundation day address
content: Ms. Pawar said that the party remained committed to creating a harmonious social environment and was willing to make political adjustments for upholding it.

title: Congress MLC Naseer Ahmed faces disqualification from being a legislator as NCLT declares him ‘bankrupt’
content: The NCLT has appointed Insolvency Professional, Ravindra Beleyur, as Bankruptcy Trustee (BT) under Section 125 of the IBC to take custody of all the assets/estates of Mr. Naseer, his wife and son for recovering the dues to the creditors

title: Vizag steel plant explosion | Data reveals major lacunae in reporting industrial accidents
content: The inconsistencies in the data compiled by the Labour Bureau and the DGFASLI point to severe lapses in reporting of industrial accidents

title: Maharashtra govt takes cognisance of aggregated cab drivers arm-twisting customers for tips
content: Over the past few days, incidents of drivers demanding additional tips or extra payments after accepting rides and cancelling the trips if such payments were not made, were being reported, the officials said.

title: Supreme Court stays adverse Allahbad High Court order against senior IAS officer
content: “Superior officers must be held accountable for the conduct and performance of their subordinates as it is their professional and administrative responsibility to ensure effective delivery of public services,” says the High Court

title: Will remove AFSPA from Northeast barring one or two States next year: Shah
content: Amit Shah terms MoU signed between Centre, Assam, and Nagaland on mineral oil explorations a “historic moment” and says it removes the last hurdle in Prime Minister Modi’s vision of a developed Northeast.

title: ED files protest petition against DVAC’s attempt to close ₹27.90 crore bribery case against former Minister Vaithilingam
content: The central agency says money laundering investigation conducted by it had revealed proceeds of crime to the tune of ₹100 crore and so the State agency must not be allowed to close the corruption case

title: U.S. attacks third ship with Indian seafarers; crew safe
content: External Affairs Ministry says ‘deeply worried’ by attacks but blames conflict in the region; Congress asks Prime Minister Modi to take responsibility while CPI(M) urges strong condemnation by government

title: Maharashtra police register case against comedian Pranit More, others over ‘₹370 biryani’ remark
content: National Commission for Women (NCW) also summoned Mr. More and Mr. Jangra, taking suo motu cognisance of media reports and a viral video of the show

title: Congress to mount legal, political battle over rejection of Meenakshi Natarajan’s Rajya Sabha nomination
content: Party calls the issue a case of ‘seat chori’ by ‘the BJP-EC jugalbandi’; the party plans a nation-wide agitation on inflation, unemployment, NEET paper leak, CBSE OSM row, and other issues; protest campaign likely to begin by the end of June

title: France backs ‘Make in India’ in defence, signals new model for Rafale deal ahead of Modi visit
content: French diplomatic sources say future defence cooperation with India will be aligned with the ‘Make in India’ initiative and based on an equal partnership. They express optimism about the prospects of enhanced cooperation in the nuclear sector, citing recent legislative reforms in India.

title: BJP’s Rajya Sabha candidates in Madhya Pradesh elected unopposed; Election Commission yet to decide on Congress plea
content: Congress says rejection of Meenakshi Natarajan’s nomintaion was “unconstitutional”, accuses BJP of “seat theft”

title: Visakhapatnam chief engineer among three Indians killed in attack on tanker off Oman
content: “He was the sole earning member of our family,” says Suresh’s wife Bhargavi, a homemaker, who has two children studying in Classes IX and VI and expresses concern about their education and future

title: Trinamool Congress MP Prakash Chik Barik resigns from Rajya Sabha
content: I will work as per CM Suvendu Adhikari’s instructions in the future; I will work for the development, says Prakash Chik Barik

title: Kerala HC seeks report on steps to declog canals, drains in Kochi
content: Collector-led panel asked to submit a report by June 18; report to detail the progress of works and preventive measures being taken

title: Despite odd timings, football fans all set to raise the roof
content: Supporters of several football giants are preparing for late-night and early-morning kick-offs as the World Cup gets under way

title: Central, coastal A.P. receive season’s first spell of heavy rainfall
content: NTR district records widespread and very heavy rainfall during the 24 hours ending Thursday morning, while the neighbouring districts of Krishna, Guntur, Bapatla also receives widespread heavy rainfall

title: Two held for luring job seekers into cyber scam network
content: The Cyber Crime Wing issued an advisory to the public asking them to verify all overseas job offers only through authorised recruitment agencies and official government channels

title: Tinkering laboratory at school campus in Ranipet inaugurated
content: The laboratory will serve as a valuable platform and offer meaningful exposure to students not only within the school campus but also to those in the district

title: Farmer’s house near Chengam burgled
content: The police said that on their return from a temple festival, the family noticed damage to the main door of the house and valuables missing

title: CM’s promise on vehicle modification in Kerala raises many doubts
content: V.D. Satheesan’s recent assurance that the State govt. will permit ‘non-dangerous’ vehicle modifications was warmly welcomed by automobile enthusiasts. However, when the MVD came up with a list of permitted modifications, there was disappointment all around. A reality check on the legal and practical aspects of the proposal

title: Vellore Corporation to plant native saplings on Palar river bank to prevent waterlogging
content: As part of rejuvenation of the landfill site space, saplings of native species will be planted to restore soil fertility

title: Major reshuffle in police force in Kerala; P. Vijayan is ADGP (Law and Order)
content: S. Sreejith, Additional Director General of Police, Headquarters, has been transferred and posted as ADGP (Prisons and Correctional Services)

title: 'Europeans sell weapons used to attack India': Jaishankar clears New Delhi's stance on Russian oil
content: External affairs minister S Jaishankar pushed back against European criticism of India's Russia policy, highlighting that European weapons have been used against India for years. He defended India's energy choices, revealing the US encouraged New Delhi to buy Russian oil in 2022 to stabilize global markets.

title: Jaspal Rana, renowned Indian shooting coach and Asiad gold medallist, dies at 49
content: Renowned Indian shooter and coach Jaspal Rana passed away at 49 after falling ill following the ISSF World Cup in Munich. A former Asian Games gold medallist, Rana was instrumental in nurturing generations of shooting stars, including Saurabh Chaudhary and Manu Bhaker. His impactful coaching career, recognised with the Dronacharya Award, leaves an irreplaceable void in Indian sport.

title: Google donates $50mn to train 300K workers, CEO says infrastructure is key
content: Google is investing $50 million through Google.org to train 300,000 American workers for skilled trade jobs like electricians, welders, and plumbers. This initiative aims to bolster the nation's digital economy by supporting the physical infrastructure built and maintained by these essential workers. The funding will empower various labor unions and trade associations across more than 20 states.

title: Alexia Putellas: How a little girl from Camp Nou became a football immortal
content: After 14 years at Futbol Club Barcelona Femení, their captain and most decorated player, Alexia Putellas, wistfully announced her departure from the club. “It has been a perfect story,” she said in her message to her fans, and one that deserves to be told over and over again.

title: Elon Musk's SpaceX raises $75 billion in record IPO debut: 10 things to know
content: SpaceX is poised for a historic Wall Street debut, aiming to raise $75 billion and achieve a valuation of nearly $1.8 trillion. The company, now encompassing rocket launches, satellite internet, and AI, has seen significant revenue growth but reported a net loss. Institutional investors are showing strong demand for the offering.

title: Nigerian woman jumps to death as Delhi cops uncover suspected meth lab in Burari
content: A dramatic police raid in Delhi's Burari uncovered a suspected meth lab, leading to the tragic death of a Nigerian national who allegedly jumped from a fourth-floor balcony. Authorities found extensive chemicals and narcotics, including MDMA and heroin, following the arrest of several African nationals. The operation revealed a sophisticated manufacturing and storage facility.

title: Palantir CEO’s message to tech CEO: Don’t be surprised if your employees turn against you
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp cautions tech leaders against publicizing AI-driven job cuts, warning it could provoke backlash from employees, voters, and policymakers. He suggests such pronouncements risk fueling opposition and strengthening calls for tech regulation. Karp himself acknowledges Palantir uses AI for efficiency but plans to boost revenue without significant workforce expansion.

title: Trust Shafali, the bowler: Nasser offers 'safety valve' to India's balance problem
content: Team India eyes T20 World Cup glory in England, led by Harmanpreet Kaur. With key all-rounders injured, the team faces a selection dilemma: bolster batting or add bowling depth. Former captain Nasser Hussain advocates for a stronger batting lineup, emphasising fearless, aggressive play to secure above-par scores and chase the trophy.

title: Teen who flagged CBSE flaws lands full-time job at IIT-Kanpur
content: Earlier this year, as the board rolled out on-screen marking (OSM) system— under which examiners grade scanned answer sheets online — Adhikary began probing the publicly accessible parts of the set-up. He says he found serious loopholes: a master password sitting in plain sight in the portal’s code, an OTP check that ran on the user’s own browser and a flaw that let one user retrieve another’s records.

title: Meta 8,000 layoffs hit managers hardest, & CEO Zuckerberg's 2023 warning now makes sense
content: Meta's 8,000-person layoff on May 20 hit middle managers and software engineers hardest, with public filings showing more than 1,400 managers cut, nearly half of them software engineering managers. The data has reopened CEO Mark Zuckerberg's 2023 line that he didn't want "managers managing managers" at Meta. With AI capex hitting $145 billion this year, the company is now reshaping its org chart around smaller teams and individual builders.

title: Kerala SSLC revaluation result 2026 out - check here
content: Kerala Pareeksha Bhavan has announced the Kerala SSLC Revaluation Result 2026. Students seeking a review of their answer scripts can now access updated scores online. This process ensures fairness in grading. To check your result, visit the official SSLC portal, enter your details, and submit. Review your scorecard carefully and report any discrepancies promptly to your school or the board.

title: Who is Gilberto Mora? Teen sensation breaks 96-year-old record at FIFA World Cup
content: Seventeen-year-old Gilberto Mora made history at the Estadio Azteca, becoming the youngest Mexican to ever play in a FIFA World Cup. Entering the match in the 65th minute, Mora, at 17 years and 240 days, broke a record that stood since 1930. His debut marks a significant moment for Mexican football, highlighting his rapid rise as a promising talent.

title: 'Homemakers are nation builders': For payout, SC pegs housewife ‘income’ at Rs 30,000 a month
content: A Supreme Court bench of Justices Sanjay Karol and N K Singh said women were largely responsible for the preparation of human capital on which the dreams of being the world’s largest economy, among other things, rested. The bench said if the compensation was not paid within three months, the interest rate would increase to 9%, and six months later, it would go up to 12% per annum. It also listed 123 accident compensation claim cases, which were dealt with by a bench led by Justice Karol.

title: Serena's comeback ends after just one match as injury cuts short Queen's Club run
content: Serena Williams' highly anticipated return to professional tennis was cut short at Queen's Club due to her partner Victoria Mboko's knee injury. The duo had secured an opening-round win, but Mboko's withdrawal forced them out of the quarter-finals. Serena Williams is scheduled to compete in the Berlin Open next week.

title: Govt bars industries from buying petrol, diesel at pumps; mandates bulk purchase route
content: Government has restricted industrial, commercial, and institutional users from buying petrol and diesel at retail fuel stations. This move aims to curb abnormal demand growth, particularly in diesel, driven by price differences between retail and bulk sales. The order, effective for up to 90 days, ensures equitable fuel availability for ordinary consumers.

title: 1 murder, 1 warning: How twin gym shootings rocked Delhi-NCR
content: In a shocking turn of events, the Lawrence Bishnoi gang has claimed responsibility for two violent incidents: a gym shooting in New Delhi and a murder in Haryana. The Delhi shooting struck a gym frequented by celebrity Guru Randhawa, raising suspicions that it was motivated by his associations, particularly with superstar Salman Khan.

title: Women's T20 World Cup 2026 Ready Reckoner: Everything you need to know
content: England and Wales are set to host the 10th ICC Women's T20 World Cup from June 12 to July 5, featuring a record 12 teams. Defending champions New Zealand and six-time winners Australia are among the strong contenders. This expanded tournament boasts a record prize pool, promising intense competition across seven venues.

title: SpaceX IPO: Could Elon Musk become world's first trillionaire? Decoding the math
content: Elon Musk may soon become the world's first trillionaire. This is due to the upcoming initial public offering of his company SpaceX. The IPO is expected to be the largest in history. If valuations hold true, Musk's personal fortune could surge past the trillion-dollar mark. This event could reshape global wealth rankings and create new millionaires.

title: 3 killed, several injured after fire engulfs building in Delhi's Tughlakabad Extension
content: Tragedy struck Tughlakabad Extension as a devastating fire in a residential building claimed at least three lives early Friday. Firefighters battled the blaze in the narrow lane, successfully rescuing six individuals. The inferno, believed to have started from vehicles inside, was brought under control after intense efforts, with rescue operations continuing.

title: Fans can watch the FIFA World Cup 2026 opening ceremony live worldwide
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 opening ceremony will feature three major celebrations across Mexico, Canada, and the United States, making it one of the biggest tournament launches in history. Artists including Shakira, Burna Boy, J Balvin, Tyla, Michael Bublé, and Nora Fatehi are set to perform. Fans in India can watch the ceremonies live on Unite8 Sports channels and Zee5. FIFA president Gianni Infantino called the event “a truly global celebration” uniting music, culture, and football.

title: Roger Federer’s luxury car collection reflects his taste for speed and style
content: Roger Federer still has eyes on him; he has been on the Mercedes-Benz radar since forever and is an embodiment of a luxury lifestyle. He has an array of beautiful sports cars, superior SUVs and luxury cars that beautifully depict his sophisticated personality. He's not a crazy car enthusiast and wants his garage to be appreciated by tennis fans for its performance and usability, which is why his collection is one of the most loved in tennis.

title: Shakira’s World Cup 2026 performance sparked mixed reactions from fans
content: Shakira and Burna Boy opened the 2026 FIFA World Cup at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City with a live performance of the official tournament song “Dai Dai.” While many fans praised Shakira’s stage presence and the show’s energy, others criticized the vocals, choreography, and sound quality. The performance sparked major debate online ahead of Mexico’s opening match against South Africa.

title: 'Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli can ... ': Anil Kumble's message to Shubman Gill
content: Anil Kumble believes Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli's experience will be vital for India's 2027 ODI World Cup campaign, offering crucial support to captain Shubman Gill. Kumble praised Gill's IPL leadership and highlighted how the veterans can guide him through high-pressure situations, easing his burden.

title: Microsoft warns staff: Don't touch Claude Fable 5, lawyers are still reading fine print
content: Microsoft has blocked its own employees from using Anthropic's newly launched Claude Fable 5 inside GitHub Copilot, citing unresolved concerns over a 30-day data retention policy tied to the Mythos-class model. The Verge reports Microsoft's legal team is still reviewing the changes, even as paying Copilot and Foundry customers already have access. Other Claude models remain available internally under Zero Data Retention rules. Anthropic has not offered a workaround.

title: Adopt ‘targeted’ delimitation for 170 LS seats: Economic Advisory Council to PM Modi
content: According to the model proposed, Kerala and Tamil Nadu will account for 22 of the 59 proposed two-way splits. In cases of three-way splits, the lion’s share will be in UP (17), followed by Maharashtra (12), Bihar (10), and Bengal (10). As a result, the number of seats in southern states is expected to rise — in Telangana from 17 to 26, Andhra from 25 to 38, Karnataka from 28 to 42, TN from 39 to 59, and Kerala from 20 to 30.

title: After record-breaking IPL season, Sooryavanshi told to focus on the next 2-3 years
content: Veteran umpire Virender Sharma sees immense potential in young batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, highlighting his natural talent and strength. Sharma believes Sooryavanshi's consistent IPL performances, including a record-breaking 776 runs in IPL 2026, are strong indicators of future international success, predicting he will achieve wonders for India.

title: Oil prices slip below $90: Brent, WTI tumble after Trump announces ‘ending war with Iran’
content: Oil prices dropped below $90 per barrel as US President Donald Trump announced a framework agreement with Iran, signaling an end to the conflict and the expected reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. This development follows a week of heightened tensions and Iran's earlier threat to close the vital shipping lane, which had previously kept oil prices elevated.

title: Julian Quinones, who outscored Ronaldo, makes history with first goal of 2026 FIFA WC
content: Julian Quiñones made FIFA World Cup history by scoring the opening goal of the 2026 tournament as Mexico defeated South Africa 2-0. The CONCACAF player's strike, his first of the competition, came in the ninth minute at the Estadio Azteca. This historic goal followed his impressive performance in the Saudi Pro League, where he outscored Cristiano Ronaldo.

title: 3 Indians confirmed killed in US strike on tanker off Oman; 3rd vessel attacked
content: MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said two of the three vessels that came under attack were subject to sanctions administered by the US treasury's office of foreign assets control, while the Settebello had been classified as non-compliant. OFAC acts against vessels involved in violating US sanctions on the sale of Iranian and Russian oil.

title: Biggest-ever IPO: Retail investors line up, bid over $70 billion for SpaceX
content: Retail investors are expected to be allocated at least 20% of the available shares, the people said. At a $75 billion IPO size, which would be the largest on record, that allocation would leave the bulk of demand from individual investors unfulfilled. Leaving large numbers of Elon Musk’s fans empty-handed in the IPO would likely amplify demand for the shares once they begin trading.

title: Mexico’s World Cup opener turns chaotic with three red cards in 2-0 win
content: Mexico launched their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign with a commanding 2-0 victory over South Africa at Estadio Azteca. Goals from Julián Quiñones and Raúl Jiménez secured the win, but three red cards stole the spotlight in a chaotic opener. Mexico dominated possession throughout, while South Africa's discipline collapsed, leaving them with an uphill battle ahead.

title: SC to hear Natarajan’s plea against rejection of Rajya Sabha candidature today
content: Senior advocate AM Singhvi sought urgent hearing and stay of declaration of results at least for 24 hours, saying this would facilitate effective hearing before the court to adjudicate validity of the returning officer’s decision to reject Natarajan’s candidature. The plea for urgent mentioning was opposed by senior advocate Mukul Rohatgi who said the only option open for Natarajan is to file an election petition after the declaration of results on Thursday.

title: 'He is so famous': Buttler explains why Sooryavanshi's hype is bigger than Sachin's
content: Fifteen-year-old batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is set to make his India debut against Ireland and will also tour England. His remarkable IPL 2026 performance has generated significant buzz, with former England captain Jos Buttler comparing the modern-day exposure to Sachin Tendulkar's rise. Sooryavanshi's inclusion is a deserved reward for his exceptional batting numbers.

title: 'Do It When Suits Them, Don't When It Doesn't': S Jaishankar On US Tariffs
content: India became a major buyer of Russian oil after the US imposed sanctions on Russia in 2022 for invading Ukraine and capped the price of its oil.

title: One Year Of Air India Crash: Questions And Grief Remain For Victim Families
content: The Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner was flying from Ahmedabad to London Gatwick when it crashed shortly after taking off from Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport on June 12. There were 230 passengers and 12 crew members on board.

title: Cyberabad Police Cracks 8 kg Gold Heist In 72 Hours, 3 Arrested
content: Police solve high-value villa theft in Cyberabad recovering 7.8 kg gold and arresting three suspects.

title: Not Google, Consumers Are Asking AI Before Shopping. Brands Are Scrambling
content: A behavioural shift in consumer is forcing companies to rethink decade-old digital marketing playbooks. Traditional SEO may lose relevance soon.

title: Women Earn Rs 73 For Every Rs 100 Made By Men, Contribute 18% To GDP: Report
content: Women now account for nearly a quarter of India's mutual fund investors and 28 per cent of registered SIP accounts.

title: US-Iran War Live: Trump Cancels Iran Strikes After 'High-Level Approval' Of Talks
content: US-Israel-Iran War News Live Updates: Trump said key points of a potential agreement have been worked out with support from several countries, raising hopes of a deal soon.

title: Rs 547 Crore Medical Hub To Replace Burnt Buildings At Air India Crash Site
content: The mess and canteen building, that was impacted by the crash, will be rebuilt at a cost of Rs 10 crore as a ground plus one structure that will accommodate around 800 students.

title: Bengal Politics LIVE Updates: 19 Trinamool MPs Sign Dissent Note, Seek Separate Seats In Lok Sabha
content: West Bengal Politics LIVE Updates: Crisis within the party escalated dramatically with a full-blown rebellion in the legislature and Parliament wings.

title: A Year After Air India Crash: Fear, Trust And Heavy Toll Of Flight AI171
content: While passengers hold diverse viewpoints, the flight crew remains mostly united in their belief that aviation is still the safest mode of travel, adhering strictly to all necessary protocols.

title: "I Worry Something Will Go Wrong": Anthropic CEO On AI
content: Anthropic co-founder and CEO Dario Amodei is very vocal about his fears that AI could get out of hand.

title: Andhra Man Among 3 Killed In Ship Attack, Leaves Behind 2 Young Children
content: The Palau-flagged tanker MT Settebello was operating near the Strait of Hormuz when it was struck during a US military operation. Of the 24 Indian crew members on board, 21 were rescued.

title: US Imposes Sanctions On Cuba State Oil Company Amid Energy Crisis
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio asserted that key assets of the company, known as Cupet, were "unlawfully expropriated from American owners years ago."

title: Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha Dies At 47, Three Years After Sudden Collapse
content: She was suffering from an abdominal infection and "her condition continued to worsen", the Bureau of the Royal Household said in a statement, until she "passed away peacefully" on Thursday evening.

title: Opening Of Canada-US Gordie Howe Bridge Delayed Amid Unresolved Issues
content: The 1.5-mile-long (2.4-kilometer-long) Gordie Howe International Bridge spans the Detroit River and connects the Motor City with Windsor, Ontario.

title: Iran Says 'Text Not Final' After Trump Claims Khamenei Approved Peace Deal
content: Trump said his understanding was that the Iranian Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, had given his approval to the proposal.

title: SpaceX IPO Raises $75 Billion In Biggest Debut Of All Time
content: SpaceX is the first of three major IPOs expected to capitalise on stock investors' appetite for the leading AI companies.

title: Potential heir to Thai throne, Princess Bajrakitiyabha dies at 47 after 3-year coma
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. title: Scientists use rice's hidden weakness to create self-changing material
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More
